reviews.review_title: This is what VR is made for
reviews.review_description:
Xploit doesnt just showcase what virtual reality can do, it reminds you why VR exists in the first place. From the moment the experience begins, it grabs you with a sense of presence that feels bold, physical, immersive, and vry fun. 

The game doesn’t settle for letting you watch action happen around you; it demands that you live it. The play area becomes your arena, and Xploit uses every inch of it with purpose. Movement isn’t a gimmick here, itis the core of the experience. You dodge, you lean, you jump, you react instinctively, and suddenly you realize you’re not *playing a game*; you’re inside a world that refuses to let you stay still.

It’s the kind of VR that leaves you breathless, sweaty, and absolutely convinced that this medium still has unexplored heights.

developer_response.body:
Hi nutcracker.189610,

Thank you so much for this incredible review. Reading your words is exactly the kind of motivation that keeps us building Xploit. Our goal has always been to create an experience where movement isn’t just an option but the heart of the gameplay, and it’s amazing to hear that this is exactly how it feels on your side.

Thank you for taking the time to share your experience — and for stepping into the arena with such enthusiasm. There’s much more to come!

Best regards
The Xploit ZERO team

reviews.review_description:
As a long time user of my beloved Golf 5, I was invited to try the early versions of Xploit before it was released. I have to admit that, due to lack of time and because I didn’t really understand the game very well (it didn’t have a tutorial back then), I didn’t pay much attention to it. Still, to support the development team, I bought the game on release day.

I have to say the game is entertaining me a lot. It’s a super dynamic and physically demanding experience, and incredibly engaging. Every level (I’m still on level 3 on easy difficulty) requires constant movement (those missiles are evil) and offers a very exciting challenge, both physically and strategically. On the first day I played until I drained the battery of my Quest 3.

Highly recommended,but be careful, it’s extremely addictive! 😁

Important: make sure you play in a sufficiently large space. I started in a small area and it didn’t go well. Now I play in a space of about 8 ftX11 ft, and it’s perfect.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for this amazing review, Squid4318!

It means a lot to us that someone who tried the very early versions came back for the full release — and even more that you’re now having such a great time with the game.
We’re really happy to hear that the new tutorial, the improved levels, and the more dynamic gameplay clicked with you. Xploit ZERO is designed to be fast, physical, and strategically intense, so knowing you drained your Quest 3 battery on day one is the best compliment we could get.

And yes… those missiles are evil. By design.

Best regards,
The Xploit ZERO team

reviews.review_description: Xploit zero turns your room into an action packed battlefield full of strategy and immersion. The gameplay is fun and addictive, mixing tower defense with real movement. It feels fresh and different from other VR games. Highly recommended. mazing mixed reality experience.

developer_response.body:
Hi fitzroy.w.2023,

Thank you so much for your review! We’re really happy to hear that you’re enjoying Xploit.ZERO. The game is still in Early Access, so there are plenty of improvements and surprises on the way. We can’t wait to share what’s coming next.

Best regards,
The Xploit.ZERO Team

reviews.review_description:
Xploit zero is a tower defence that mixes things up to adapt it to MR. Right from the start, you'll be moving around your room collecting memory and trying to avoid your turrets getting wipped out.And if you decide to play it in hard mode... Oh boy, you'll actually have to stop and think about your straregy to beat each level.
Honestly, the game is pretty addictive, and you end up challenging yourself when things get tough.

developer_response.body:
Hi rociTriple,

Thank you so much for taking the time to share your thoughts!

We're really glad to hear that Xploit ZERO clicked with you. Moving around your space at full speed is exactly the kind of MR gameplay we wanted to deliver. And yes… Hard Mode definitely forces you to stop, breathe, and rethink your strategy. It’s great to hear you enjoyed that extra layer of challenge.

Thanks for playing and for the awesome feedback!

Best regards,
The Xploit ZERO Team

reviews.review_description:
Fun and intense MR game!. You’re placing turrets around your  room while slaps flying enemies, so you end up moving a lot! it’s my new app of workout! You also need to think carefully about your turret strategy.

The whole experience feels immersive, as your room starts getting virtual while you play. And the music really stands out, with a high-energy synthwave that fits the action.

It had been a while since I'd played so much with my headset. Perfect for quick, high-energy sessions.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for your amazing review, retrostryker!

We’re really happy to hear that you’re enjoying the “cyberpunk workout” experience — that’s exactly the kind of fun, high‑energy vibe we were aiming for.

Thanks again for playing and for taking the time to share your thoughts — it really motivates us to keep improving and adding more to the experience.

Best regards
The Xploit ZERO team

reviews.review_description: Xploit Zero is a clever and energetic AR tower‑defense experience that immediately stands out for its originality. The room scanning and spatial setup are quick and intuitive, and once the action begins, enemies blend seamlessly into your real environment. The mix of turret strategy, fast movement to gather memory, and hands‑on interception of flying enemies creates a dynamic rhythm that keeps you constantly engaged. It’s well designed, surprisingly deep, and offers a refreshing twist on the genre, delivering both challenge and fun in equal measure.

developer_response.body:
Hi Mateator,

Thank you for such a thoughtful and detailed review!

Hearing that the game feels both original and well‑designed to you truly means a lot. Thanks for playing and for sharing your experience!

Best regards,
The Xploit ZERO team

reviews.review_description:
The game works like this. First, you have to scan the room, but that’s very easy on the Quest 3, you just look around. You only need to do it once, and you only have to repeat it if you change rooms. Then you need to place an element called the neurogate. I usually put it in the center of the room.
Next, a path is drawn that the enemies will follow. To defend from them, you place different types of turrets; some are more effective against certain enemies, and others work better against different ones. Memory drops from the ceiling, and you have to collect it by moving quickly from one side of the room to the other. You use that memory to build your defenses.
Red flying enemies also appear, and you have to intercept them with your hands to prevent them from destroying the turrets. Additionally, the turrets self‑destruct when they reach the end of their lifespan.
There are two difficulty levels, Normal and Hard, and a tutorial at the beginning of each stage explains how the game works. However, the tutorial can be skipped by pressing X and can also be disabled in the settings.
Original, dynamic, and very fun game.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for taking the time to write such a detailed review, pedro.165691!

We’re really glad to hear that you enjoyed the mix of turret strategy, quick movement to collect memory, and the hands‑on action of intercepting flying enemies. Hearing that you found the game original, dynamic, and very fun truly means a lot.

Thanks for playing and for sharing your experience!

Best regards,
The Xploit ZERO team

reviews.review_description: An impressively polished AR experience: you scan your room, place your towers wherever they fit best, and the action blends naturally into your space. It’s smooth, immersive, and surprisingly addictive, the more you play, the more it pulls you in.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for your review, Radnekor! 

We’re really happy to hear you enjoyed the AR experience. Our goal was to make the room‑scan and tower placement feel natural and intuitive, so it’s great to know it came through on your end.

Thanks again for playing and for taking the time to share your thoughts!

Best regards
The Xploit ZERO team

reviews.review_description:
To help you during Early Access, here’s my contribution:
- Although it’s true that the game offers many hours of gameplay, especially on hard mode, I need more levels! You could alternate calmer levels between the more intense rush levels.a bit of breathing room every now and then would be appreciated.
- I miss having information about the bonuses that some turrets may have against certain enemy types.
- Sometimes the sound cues don’t give enough information about where the aerial enemies are going to appear.
Other than that, I find it a very original, fun, and dynamic game. It will earn for suremy fifth star when I see it evolve further.

Good job!

developer_response.body:
Hi Krisblackwidow,

Thank you very much for your feedback!

Regarding the levels, there will definitely be more in the future. Adding calmer stages between the more intense rush levels is a great idea, and we’ll pass it along to the development team.

A guide is also necessary — we’re already working on one, and it will be available soon.

We’ll also share your comments about the sound cues with the team.

We hope to earn your fifth star very soon. Thanks again!

Best regards,
The Golf 5 team

reviews.review_description: The game is pretty interesting, it's got more to it than it seemed at first. For thr price, and considering it's early access, it's been a really nice suprise.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for your review, Boldir!

We’re really glad to hear that the game surprised you in a good way. There’s more depth than it first seems, and it’s great to know that came through as you played.

We truly appreciate your comment about the value for money, and since the game is still in Early Access, your feedback helps us keep improving and adding more.

Thanks again for giving it a try and for sharing your thoughts!

Best regards
The Xploit ZERO team

reviews.review_description:
I’m a veteran gamer in my 50s, and I still remember playing Civ II and having that 'just one more turn' feeling that kept me up all night.
Xploit brought that memory right back, because the 'just one more try'thought has hit me many times since I got the game. Lots of fun and incredibly addictive.

developer_response.body:
Hi FluffyOtter9400,

Thank you so much for this!

Hearing that Xploit brought back that classic “just one more turn” feeling is honestly one of the best compliments we could ever get. As veteran gamers ourselves, we know exactly what that magic feels like.

It means a lot to us that someone with your experience — and those Civilization II all‑nighters under your belt — found the game fun and addictive. That’s the highest praise.

Best regards
The Xploit ZERO team

reviews.review_description:
Xploit zero is a super fun mixed reality tower defense game that literally turns your room into a battlefield. Enemies follow a path drawn across your actual walls, floor, and ceiling, and you drop turrets wherever you’ve got space. 
You’ll be grabbing yellow  memory cards falling from above for resources and even swatting away rockets with your hands to protect your defenses, which makes the whole experience feel physical and chaotic in a good way. 
it’s original, energetic, and way more immersive than any other tower defense game.

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for the awesome review, Mills.Isa! 

We're thrilled that the mixed‑reality chaos, physical interactions, and turret‑dropping strategy all clicked for you. Hearing that it feels more immersive and energetic than other tower defense games really means a lot. Glad you had a blast!

Best regards,
The Xploit ZERO team

reviews.review_title: Is there a way to skip the tutorial?
reviews.review_description: I love this game. It would be 5 stars if there were a way to skp the tutorial. It’s annoying to have to watch it every time a level starts. Maxim

developer_response.body:
Hi Maxim, thanks for your comment! 

You can skip the tutorial by pressing the X button on your left controller.

If you want to prevent the tutorial from appearing again, simply uncheck the “TUTORIAL” box in the Settings menu.

We hope this helps, and that we can earn your fifth star.

For this and any other questions about the game, feel free to contact us at users@aaagamestudios.com or join the Xploit ZERO Discord server: https://discord.gg/rwq4FCPFBw

Best regards
The Xploit Zero team

reviews.review_description: Frantic. I❤️it.

reviews.review_title: Fun when you have enough space to play
reviews.review_description: I recommend not playing in a very small space. At first, I set up a play area that was too small, and the experience wasn’t very satisfying. The developers recommend at least 2.5 m x 2.5 m, and with a larger space (mine is 3 x 3.5 m) the game becomes really fun.

developer_response.body:
Hi VotePedro,

Thanks for sharing your experience! You’re absolutely right: having enough play space makes a huge difference. We recommend at least 2.5 × 2.5 m, and it’s great to hear the game really clicked for you once you defined that space. Enjoy!

Best regards,
The Xploit ZERO team
